recommendations.title: Reduce Vibration on Machine M3
recommendations.description: Machine M3 has 390 readings exceeding the 4.5 mm/s vibration threshold. Excessive vibration is the leading cause of soldering defects. Inspect bearings, check belt tension, and verify mounting bolts.

recommendations.title: Recalibrate Machines: M3, M7, M8, M4
recommendations.description: 8 machines show calibration drift beyond 0.15 tolerance. Calibration drift progressively degrades placement accuracy. Schedule immediate recalibration for all affected machines.
recommendations.estimated_impact: Prevent ~20% of pinhole and placement defects

recommendations.title: Improve Thermal Management System
recommendations.description: 3.0% of readings show temperature above 85.0°C. Check cooling system capacity, clean heat exchangers, and verify HVAC setpoints in production area.

recommendations.title: Investigate Anomaly Patterns on Machine M1
recommendations.description: Machine M1 shows 45.5% anomaly rate. This indicates potential systematic issues. Review sensor calibration, inspect wear components, and check control system parameters.

recommendations.title: Investigate Anomaly Patterns on Machine M3
recommendations.description: Machine M3 shows 87.7% anomaly rate. This indicates potential systematic issues. Review sensor calibration, inspect wear components, and check control system parameters.

recommendations.title: Investigate Anomaly Patterns on Machine M4
recommendations.description: Machine M4 shows 48.6% anomaly rate. This indicates potential systematic issues. Review sensor calibration, inspect wear components, and check control system parameters.

recommendations.title: Investigate Anomaly Patterns on Machine M6
recommendations.description: Machine M6 shows 46.8% anomaly rate. This indicates potential systematic issues. Review sensor calibration, inspect wear components, and check control system parameters.

recommendations.title: Investigate Anomaly Patterns on Machine M7
recommendations.description: Machine M7 shows 52.1% anomaly rate. This indicates potential systematic issues. Review sensor calibration, inspect wear components, and check control system parameters.

recommendations.title: Investigate Anomaly Patterns on Machine M8
recommendations.description: Machine M8 shows 59.9% anomaly rate. This indicates potential systematic issues. Review sensor calibration, inspect wear components, and check control system parameters.

recommendations.title: Enhance Night Shift Quality Controls
recommendations.description: Night shift has 42% higher defect rate (avg 3.92 vs 2.75). Consider: adding a quality inspector, implementing more frequent inline checks, reducing production speed by 10-15%, and improving lighting conditions.
recommendations.estimated_impact: Reduce night shift defect gap by ~40%

recommendations.title: Address Pattern: Vibration × Speed Interaction
recommendations.description: Pattern discovered: High vibration (>4 mm/s) combined with high speed (>100 units/hr) drops yield to 86.5% vs plant average of 92.0%.. Correlation strength: 0.760. Investigate and implement targeted countermeasures.

recommendations.title: Address Pattern: Vibration Mm S
recommendations.description: Pattern discovered: When Machine vibration exceeds 4.5 mm/s: 24% of defects are 'open' type. Defect rate increases by 111% (avg 5.9 vs 2.8). Correlation strength: 0.557. Investigate and implement targeted countermeasures.

recommendations.title: Mitigate Root Cause: Calibration Drift
recommendations.description: Calibration Drift contributes 37.2% to the quality issue. Calibration drift explains 37.2% of defects. Progressive drift leads to placement errors and dimensional defects. Prioritize actions targeting this factor for maximum impact.

recommendations.title: Mitigate Root Cause: Vibration (mm/s)
recommendations.description: Vibration (mm/s) contributes 29.1% to the quality issue. Machine vibration contributes 29.1% to defect occurrence. High vibration causes mechanical misalignment during soldering. Prioritize actions targeting this factor for maximum impact.
